description: Wire provider response headers through the onRequestSuccess callback chain so that Codex rate limit headers reach clearAccountError, enabling the existing proactive cooldown code to function.
steps: Step 1: Modify onRequestSuccess callback signature in src/sse/handlers/chat.ts. Change the onRequestSuccess callback from `async () => { await clearAccountError(credentials.connectionId, credentials, model); }` to `async (responseHeaders?: any) => { await clearAccountError(credentials.connectionId, credentials, model, responseHeaders); }`. This is at approximately line 239 in handleSingleModelChat where handleChatCore is called.

Step 2: Modify onRequestSuccess callback signature in src/sse/handlers/embeddings.ts. Same pattern: change from `async () => { await clearAccountError(credentials.connectionId, credentials, model); }` to `async (responseHeaders?: any) => { await clearAccountError(credentials.connectionId, credentials, model, responseHeaders); }`.
Step 3: Modify onRequestSuccess callback signature in src/sse/handlers/imageGeneration.ts. Same pattern as above for both the noAuth case and the credentialed case.

Step 4: In open-sse/handlers/chatCore.ts, add providerResponse to the sharedCtx object (it is already in scope). Specifically, after the `const sharedCtx = {...}` definition (around line 520), add `providerResponse` to it (or keep it available for the sub-handlers).

Step 5: In open-sse/handlers/chatCore/streamingHandler.ts handleStreamingResponse function, change `if (onRequestSuccess) onRequestSuccess();` to `if (onRequestSuccess) onRequestSuccess(providerResponse?.headers);`. Ensure providerResponse is passed in from the destructured params (it already is).

Step 6: In open-sse/handlers/chatCore/sseToJsonHandler.ts handleForcedSSEToJson function, change the two occurrences of `if (onRequestSuccess) await onRequestSuccess();` to `if (onRequestSuccess) await onRequestSuccess(providerResponse?.headers);`. providerResponse is already in the function params.

Step 7: In open-sse/handlers/chatCore/nonStreamingHandler.ts handleNonStreamingResponse function, change `if (onRequestSuccess) await onRequestSuccess();` to `if (onRequestSuccess) await onRequestSuccess(providerResponse?.headers);`. providerResponse is already in the function params.

Step 8: In open-sse/handlers/embeddingsCore.tsx handleEmbeddingsCore function, change `if (onRequestSuccess) { await onRequestSuccess(); }` to `if (onRequestSuccess) { await onRequestSuccess(providerResponse?.headers); }`. The variable `providerResponse` is already in scope.

Step 9: In open-sse/handlers/imageGenerationCore.ts handleImageGenerationCore function, change `if (onRequestSuccess) { await onRequestSuccess(); }` to `if (onRequestSuccess) { await onRequestSuccess(providerResponse?.headers); }`. The variable `providerResponse` is already in scope.
acceptance_criteria: TypeScript compiles without errors: npx tsc --noEmit passes
All existing unit tests pass: npm run test
The onRequestSuccess callback in all three SSE handlers (chat.ts, embeddings.ts, imageGeneration.ts) passes responseHeaders through to clearAccountError
clearAccountError receives responseHeaders from all chatCore sub-handlers (streaming, sseToJson, nonStreaming), embeddingsCore, and imageGenerationCore
The existing proactive cooldown code in clearAccountError (auth.tsx lines 963-975) will now fire when Codex response headers contain quota information
verification: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` and confirm zero errors
Run `npm run test` and confirm all tests pass
findings: All changes passed tsc --noEmit and npm run test (200 test files, 1074 tests passed). Node.js 22 installed via mise with MISE_NODE_VERIFY=false workaround for GPG issue. The mise.toml was generated but not committed as it is local env config.
